reviewed.by: REBUILT 2026-07-23 to foreground the argument explicitly (opens 'AN ARGUMENT FOR GOD' / 'Is mathematics evidence for God?'; scene 7 states the core inference; scene 8 the honest bound). Grounded in certified library/mathematics.html (CLEAN 2026-07-14). apologia-orthodoxy CLEAN: fine-tuning analog HELD — DATA conceded, MIND is the inferred/contested conclusion; never 'math proves God'; Wigner quotes handled as HIS words, not consensus/Church; scene 8 'doesn't prove God on its own… a signpost, not the whole map'; close 'the most natural reading… is that Reason wrote it' = inference; 2 clarifier candidates surfaced, 0 actioned (both leave-as-is: capital-R 'Reason' personalized by 'Giver'/'Mind'; 'miracle' bounded by 'HIS OWN WORD FOR IT'). apologia-argument SOUND (0 BREAK/0 WEAK/3 POLISH): APPLIED polish 1 (dropped scare-quotes on 'useless' → 'useless-looking', can't read as an attributed 1854 quote) + polish 3 ('simplest'→'most natural reading', exact essay wording); polish 2 (survivorship-bias hedge) left as-is per the agent's lean — reel positive-case remit + explicit scope bound + linked essay carry it. voiceover: Here's one of the most fascinating arguments for God's existence — and it comes from mathematics. Look closely at the universe and you find something strange: it obeys mathematics, with eerie, decimal-point precision. Why should it? Take one example. In 1854 a mathematician dreamed up a bizarre, useless-looking curved geometry — pure abstraction, no use in sight. Sixty years later it turned out to be the exact language of Einstein's gravity. And this keeps happening. The math even runs ahead of us: in 1962 it predicted a particle nobody had ever seen — and two years later, they found it. A Nobel physicist, Eugene Wigner, called this 'the unreasonable effectiveness of mathematics,' and admitted there is no rational explanation for it. His own words for it? 'A wonderful gift which we neither understand nor deserve.' He reached for one word: miracle. So here's the argument. A universe written in elegant mathematics makes far more sense if a rational Mind wrote it than if it's a blind accident. Now — be honest. This doesn't prove God all by itself; it's one clue in a bigger case, a signpost, not the whole map. But a gift implies a giver. And the most natural reading of a cosmos written in reason is that Reason wrote it.
source: library/mathematics.html (certified content-review CLEAN 2026-07-14). Rebuilt to foreground the ARGUMENT explicitly: the essay's P1 (deep predictive applicability of math is a striking fact) → P2 (far more expected if reality is the product of a rational mind than if not) → conclusion (some EVIDENCE, not proof, of a Mind behind the world; inference to the best explanation, one strand in a cumulative case). Facts: Riemann 1854 → Einstein's general relativity; Gell-Mann omega-minus predicted 1962 / found 1964; Wigner (Nobel) 'unreasonable effectiveness' + 'no rational explanation' + 'a wonderful gift which we neither understand nor deserve' (verbatim). Scope bound kept intact (signpost / best explanation, NEVER 'math proves God'; the DATA is conceded, the MIND is the inferred, contested conclusion — fine-tuning-rule analog). Positive-case only.

sub.t: One of the most fascinating clues in modern science.
